---
name: TanStack route codegen + vitest OOM workarounds
description: New /routes file fails tsc until routeTree.gen.ts is regenerated (restart the dev workflow); full vitest OOMs — run per-batch in separate processes.
---

# Two recurring dev-tooling gotchas

## New TanStack route → tsc fails until route tree regenerates
Adding a `src/routes/*.tsx` file makes `createFileRoute("/x")` and `<Link to="/x">`
fail `bunx tsc --noEmit` with "not assignable to keyof FileRoutesByPath" /
"is not assignable to type '…'". `routeTree.gen.ts` is generated by the TanStack
router vite plugin, NOT by tsc. Cure: **restart the `Start application` workflow**
(runs vite → regenerates `src/routeTree.gen.ts`), then re-run tsc. It is not a
code error; do not "fix" the route file.

## Full `bunx vitest run` gets OOM-killed (exit -1, no output)
Running the whole suite at once is intermittently killed (memory pressure, worse
with the dev server running). `--no-file-parallelism` / `--pool` flags don't
reliably help (and `--poolOptions.*` CLI flags are rejected as unknown). Reliable
workaround: run the suite as **several separate `vitest run <glob…>` invocations**
(each is a fresh process that frees memory between batches), e.g. loop over groups
of filename prefixes. Individual files / small batches pass fine.
